planned_changes: Generate a plain-text formula dump file named privacy_release_4_formulas.txt that lists sheet name, cell address, and exact formula text for all key cells in remediation_analysis and decision_logic (and any other computed cells referenced in the memo).
Export the evidence_table sheet as evidence_table.csv containing the computed intermediate numeric values for each record and remediation option.

Update expert_memo.md to include an explicit budget arithmetic section with: 'Total remediation cost = <X>; max_budget = 121200; Budget check => PASS/FAIL' for the remediation analysis and note any rounding rules used.

If any formula text was programmatically assembled (SpreadsheetML/XML), also include the small reproducible script or manifest used to produce those formulas (e.g., formula_manifest.json or a short script) so a reviewer can re-run parsing in their environment.
Run a local check to confirm the new text/CSV exports are present and reference them in the submission manifest and final_message.txt.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Submission met the artifact contract and the memo documents rules, formulas, intermediate values, and the decision pipeline. Verifier flagged a residual verification gap: the .xlsx could not be parsed programmatically (openpyxl unavailable), so per-cell formulas and formula text were not machine-validated. To remove this gap and increase reproducibility, provide a plain-text export of key formulas (or enable programmatic parsing) and make budget pass/fail calculations explicit in the memo.
